channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan illegal immigrants detained at Guantánamo Bay are flown back home
item.description: Flights that left from Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, Thursday transported nearly 200 illegal immigrants detained on the island back to Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP sues 3 Trump administration officials, citing freedom of speech
item.description: The Associated Press is suing three Trump administration officials over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration looks to slash HUD workers tackling the housing crisis
item.description: The Trump administration's proposal to cut half of federal workers at the nation's housing agency is targeting employees who support disaster recovery, rental subsidies, discrimination investigations and first-time homebuyers, according to two documents obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia woman says she'll 'never fully recover' after IVF mix-up leads to her losing custody of baby
item.description: A Georgia's woman's intimate journey with IVF left her feeling "violated" after she gave birth to someone else's child, a lawsuit claims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Spring break warning: Most unlikely American tourist hot spots emerge as deadly risk
item.description: The Caribbean, a popular spring break destination for Americans, is relatively safe, but the State Department is warning U.S. tourists to stay away from certain countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Kentucky AG Daniel Cameron launches Senate bid
item.description: Prior to becoming Kentucky’s first Black attorney general, Cameron served as legal counsel to McConnell, the state’s longest serving senator, for two years.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas DPS arrests several illegal aliens wanted for sexual assault crimes
item.description: The Texas Department of Public Safety made several arrest over the past few weeks, including several who were in the country illegally and wanted for child sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CA gas prices highest in nation, Bay Area averaging $5-plus per gallon
item.description: (KRON) -- California gas prices are known to be significantly higher compared to other states in the country. On Thursday, the American Automobile Association (AAA) announced California gas is officially the priciest in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione case timeline, from UnitedHealthcare CEO killing to court dates
item.description: According to police, Luigi Mangione planned this shooting and came from Pennsylvania to make it happen.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: 53-car pileup leaves 1 dead in North Carolina, adding to hundreds of snow-related wrecks
item.description: It's a mess out there from the Sandhills to the eastern stretches of the CBS 17 viewing area, causing dozens of wrecks along major and secondary roads in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drake suprises pregnant fan with VIP tickets and $30,000
item.description: Drake gifted one fan in a major way during a Sydney show on his Anita Max Win Tour in Australia on Wednesday night. A pregnant fan, identified as Tiana Henderson, was sitting in the pit holding a sign that read "I'm 20 weeks pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: Trader Joe’s has issued a recall of its Organic Acai Bowls over the potential that the product may contain plastic, the company announced late last week. A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product had been removed from sale as of Feb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hanceville police chief, 4 officers and spouse arrested in investigation following dispatcher's overdose death
item.description: Autopsy results show a Hanceville dispatcher found dead in his office in August 2024 died from the combined toxic effects of multiple drugs.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9
